title: padus serotina var. asplenifolia
intro: Padus serotina var. asplenifolia, also known as Alderleaf Gooseberry or Gooseberry Alder, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its compound leaves and small white flowers. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, derived from its fruit, leaves, and bark. The health benefits of Padus serotina var. asplenifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the fruit, leaves, and bark, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions.
